evals.prompt: We're getting 500 support tickets/month about auth issues. Should we rip out our custom JWT auth and migrate to Auth0, or invest 2 sprints hardening what we have? Team is 8 engineers, 2 have auth domain knowledge.
evals.expected_output: A structured 6-round adversarial debate with Architect/Operator/CTO personas producing a decision document or ADR with verdict, action items, and debate metrics.

assertions.text: Output contains all 6 rounds (Round 1 through Round 6)
Architect, Operator, and CTO personas all appear and are distinct
Every challenge in Round 2 has a severity rating (critical/significant/minor)
Round 2 challenges are categorized
Round 4 references specific mitigations from Round 3, not just repeating Round 2
Round 6 contains one of the 5 valid verdicts
Metrics table present with all 8 metrics filled in
Concrete action items with who/what/when
Round 5 modifies the proposal or justifies keeping it
Round 5 includes fallback plans for surviving challenges

evals.prompt: Our PostgreSQL instance is hitting 80% CPU at peak. The data team wants us to move the analytics workload to ClickHouse. We have 3TB of event data, 50M events/day, and the current queries take 15-30 seconds. Should we do it?
evals.expected_output: A structured 6-round adversarial debate producing an ADR about the ClickHouse migration with specific failure modes, stress-tested mitigations, and a clear verdict.

assertions.text: Output contains all 6 rounds (Round 1 through Round 6)
Architect, Operator, and CTO personas all appear and are distinct
Every challenge in Round 2 has a severity rating (critical/significant/minor)
Round 2 challenges are categorized
Round 4 references specific mitigations from Round 3, not just repeating Round 2
Round 6 contains one of the 5 valid verdicts
Metrics table present with all 8 metrics filled in
Concrete action items with who/what/when
Round 5 modifies the proposal or justifies keeping it
Round 5 includes fallback plans for surviving challenges

evals.prompt: We're a monolith on Rails serving 200 RPS. Product wants to launch a real-time collaboration feature (think multiplayer editing). Should we extract a WebSocket service in Go/Elixir, or add ActionCable to the monolith? We need to ship in 8 weeks.
evals.expected_output: A structured 6-round adversarial debate about the architecture choice with timeline pressure as a key constraint, producing a decision with concrete action items.

assertions.text: Output contains all 6 rounds (Round 1 through Round 6)
Architect, Operator, and CTO personas all appear and are distinct
Every challenge in Round 2 has a severity rating (critical/significant/minor)
Round 2 challenges are categorized
Round 4 references specific mitigations from Round 3, not just repeating Round 2
Round 6 contains one of the 5 valid verdicts
Metrics table present with all 8 metrics filled in
Concrete action items with who/what/when
Round 5 modifies the proposal or justifies keeping it
Round 5 includes fallback plans for surviving challenges
